SvelteKit with TypeScript and Vite configuration - TailwindCSS for utility-first styling - ESLint + Prettier for code quality - Testing framework setup (Vitest + Testing Library) - Hot reload development environment **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] SvelteKit application runs with hot reload - [ ] TypeScript configuration supports strict mode - [ ] TailwindCSS integrated with design system tokens - [ ] ESLint rules enforce code quality standards - [ ] Unit testing framework configured and working - [ ] Build process generates optimized production bundle **Project Structure:** ``` src/ ├── lib/ │ ├── components/ # Reusable UI components │ ├── stores/ # Svelte stores for state management │ ├── types/ # TypeScript type definitions │ └── utils/ # Utility functions ├── routes/ # SvelteKit routing │ ├── +layout.svelte # App shell layout │ └── +page.svelte # Main app page ├── app.html # HTML template └── app.d.ts # App type definitions ``` --- #### GUI-015: WCAG Audit & Contrast Theme Pass **Points: 2 | Priority: High** **Description:** Implement accessibility foundation with WCAG 2.2 AA compliance and proper color contrast. **Technical Requirements:** - Color palette with 4.5:1 contrast ratio minimum - Keyboard navigation support for all interactive elements - Screen reader compatibility with semantic HTML - Focus management and visible focus indicators - Reduced motion support for accessibility **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Color contrast ratios meet WCAG 2.2 AA standards - [ ] All interactive elements keyboard accessible - [ ] Screen reader announces content changes appropriately - [ ] Focus indicators clearly visible and consistent - [ ] Reduced motion preferences respected - [ ] Automated accessibility testing passes **Accessibility Implementation:** ```typescript // Color system with verified contrast ratios export const colors = { primary: { 50: '#f0f9ff', // Background: 18.94:1 ratio 500: '#3b82f6', // Interactive: 4.5:1 ratio 900: '#1e3a8a' // Text: 12.63:1 ratio }, semantic: { success: '#059669', // 4.52:1 ratio warning: '#d97706', // 4.51:1 ratio error: '#dc2626' // 5.36:1 ratio } }; // Keyboard navigation utilities export function trapFocus(element: HTMLElement): () => void; export function announceToScreenReader(message: string): void; export function respectsReducedMotion(): boolean; ``` --- ### Epic: Visual Timeline Viewer **Story Points: 8** #### GUI-002: Visual Timeline (Read-Only) **Points: 6 | Priority: Critical** **Description:** Create the visual timeline component that displays experiment structure as an interactive timeline graph. **Technical Requirements:** - Hierarchical timeline visualization (Experiment → Timeline → Trial → Stimulus) - Zoom and pan functionality for large experiments - Interactive element selection and highlighting - Timeline synchronization with code editor - Performance optimization for complex experiments **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Timeline displays complete experiment hierarchy - [ ] Smooth zoom/pan interactions with performance <16ms - [ ] Element selection highlights corresponding code - [ ] Timeline scales gracefully from simple to complex experiments - [ ] Visual indicators for timing relationships - [ ] Responsive design works on different screen sizes **Timeline Visualization Components:** ```svelte
``` --- #### GUI-016: Timeline Performance Optimization **Points: 2 | Priority: Medium** **Description:** Optimize timeline rendering for large experiments with virtualization and efficient drawing. **Technical Requirements:** - Canvas-based rendering with viewport culling - Virtual scrolling for large timeline hierarchies - Efficient redraw algorithms - Memory usage optimization **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] 60fps rendering performance with 1000+ trial experiments - [ ] Memory usage remains stable during navigation - [ ] Viewport culling reduces rendering overhead - [ ] Smooth interactions even with complex experiments - [ ] Performance monitoring integrated --- ### Epic: Monaco Code Editor Integration **Story Points: 6** #### GUI-003: Code Pane (Monaco) **Points: 4 | Priority: Critical** **Description:** Integrate Monaco Editor with TypeScript support for ForgeScript editing. **Technical Requirements:** - Monaco Editor with TypeScript language service - Custom theme matching application design - ForgeScript API type definitions for IntelliSense - Error highlighting and diagnostics - Code formatting and syntax highlighting **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Monaco Editor renders with TypeScript support - [ ] IntelliSense provides ForgeScript API completions - [ ] Syntax highlighting for experiment code - [ ] Error diagnostics display inline - [ ] Code formatting works with Prettier - [ ] Custom theme matches application design **Monaco Integration:** ```typescript // monaco-setup.ts import * as monaco from 'monaco-editor'; import { defineForgeScriptAPI } from './forgescript-types'; export function setupMonacoEditor( container: HTMLElement, initialCode: string ): monaco.editor.IStandaloneCodeEditor { // Configure TypeScript compiler options monaco.languages.typescript.typescriptDefaults.setCompilerOptions({ target: monaco.languages.typescript.ScriptTarget.ES2020, allowNonTsExtensions: true, moduleResolution: monaco.languages.typescript.ModuleResolutionKind.NodeJs, module: monaco.languages.typescript.ModuleKind.CommonJS, noEmit: true, typeRoots: ["node_modules/@types"] }); // Add ForgeScript API types defineForgeScriptAPI(); // Create editor instance const editor = monaco.editor.create(container, { value: initialCode, language: 'typescript', theme: 'psyforge-theme', automaticLayout: true, minimap: { enabled: false }, fontSize: 14, fontFamily: '"JetBrains Mono", monospace', lineNumbers: 'on', roundedSelection: false, scrollBeyondLastLine: false, readOnly: false }); return editor; } // Custom theme monaco.editor.defineTheme('psyforge-theme', { base: 'vs', inherit: true, rules: [ { token: 'keyword', foreground: '0066cc' }, { token: 'string', foreground: '22863a' }, { token: 'comment', foreground: '6a737d', fontStyle: 'italic' } ], colors: { 'editor.background': '#ffffff', 'editor.foreground': '#24292e' } });
